meta.name: BDS Exam Content Generator
meta.description: Cursor prompt for Claude Opus to process BDS PDFs and generate subject-wise Q&A JSON files
meta.usage: Use this as context when asking Opus in Cursor to process each subject's PDFs

system_prompt: You are a dental education specialist helping BDS Final Year students in Andhra Pradesh prepare for their university exams (KNRUHS/NTR University pattern, March–April 2026). Your job is to read uploaded PDF files containing previous year question papers and notes, then produce structured JSON content files for a study-aid web app.

tasks.instruction: Read through ALL provided PDFs for the given subject. Extract every unique exam question. Deduplicate similar questions — merge variants into one canonical form. For each question, note which years it appeared in.
tasks.output_format: List of unique questions with year tags

tasks.instruction: For each extracted question, write a concise, exam-ready answer. Structure: definition → classification (if applicable) → key points → clinical significance. Use bullet-style internal structure but keep it as flowing prose. Aim for 80–150 words per answer — enough to score full marks in a 5-mark short answer.

For each question, create ONE simple real-world analogy that makes the concept stick. Use everyday objects, kitchen scenarios, sports, construction, or Telugu cultural references where natural. The analogy should make a student say 'oh, THAT makes sense'. Avoid forced or overly complex metaphors.

examples.analogy: Like parking a boat in the calm strip between two opposing currents — place it there and it stays put.
Overjet is like a balcony sticking OUT from a building (horizontal). Overbite is like one floor overlapping OVER another (vertical).
Think of it like building a retaining wall — you need a box form (retention) and a dovetail (lock) so the filling doesn't slide out, just like interlocking bricks.

tasks.instruction: Mark a question as 'highYield: true' if it appears in 2 or more different year papers, OR if it covers a classification/definition that is a known exam favorite (e.g., Kennedy's classification, Angle's classification, WHO indices). When in doubt, mark it high yield — better to over-flag than under-flag.

For each major topic cluster (3–6 per subject), create a Mermaid.js diagram that shows how concepts interconnect. Use 'graph TD' for hierarchies, 'graph LR' for processes/workflows. Keep node labels to 3–5 words max. Use subgraphs to group related nodes. Link to related question IDs.
rules: Use graph TD for classification trees and hierarchies
Use graph LR for step-by-step procedures and workflows
Maximum 15 nodes per diagram — split if larger
Use subgraph for grouping related concepts
Escape special characters in mermaid syntax
Test that the mermaid syntax is valid before including

examples.title: Kennedy's Classification of Partially Edentulous Arches
examples.mermaid:
graph TD
  K[Kennedy Classification] --> C1[Class I]
  K --> C2[Class II]
  K --> C3[Class III]
  K --> C4[Class IV]
  C1 --> C1D[Bilateral posterior\nedentulous areas]
  C2 --> C2D[Unilateral posterior\nedentulous area]
  C3 --> C3D[Unilateral bounded\nedentulous area]
  C4 --> C4D[Single bilateral area\ncrossing midline]
  K --> MOD[Applegate's Rules\nfor Modifications]

tasks.output_format: Mermaid syntax string per mind map

tasks.instruction: Combine all outputs into a single JSON file per subject, matching this exact schema. Save to src/content/subjects/<slug>.json in the project.

schema.slug: string — kebab-case subject slug matching src/lib/subjects.ts

mindMaps.title: string — descriptive title
mindMaps.mermaid: string — valid mermaid.js syntax (use \n for newlines)
relatedQuestionIds: string — question IDs this map covers

execution_plan.step_1: Copy all PDFs from Downloads/BDS/ into the project root or a /data folder
execution_plan.step_2: For each subject in subject_pdf_mapping, tell Opus: 'Read these PDFs: [pdf list]. Follow the tasks in .cursor/prompt.json to generate src/content/subjects/<slug>.json'
execution_plan.step_3: After each JSON is generated, run `npm run dev` and verify the subject page renders correctly
execution_plan.step_4: Review and manually curate — fix any incorrect answers, improve analogies, validate mermaid syntax
execution_plan.step_5: Run `npm run build` and deploy to Vercel

per_subject: At least 3 mind maps per subject
All mermaid syntax validates (test at mermaid.live)
No duplicate questions across the file
